ucs.title: [LLM] RedEcho/ShadowPad C2 beaconing to dynamic-DNS infrastructure on non-standard ports
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic RedEcho (TAG-38, PRC cluster overlapping APT41/Winnti) C2 fingerprint reported by Recorded Future against Indian power-sector targets: ShadowPad implants beaconing over TLS to actor-acquired dynamic-DNS hostnames (T1583.001 + T1568) on non-HTTPS ports (T1571) with asymmetrically-encrypted application-layer C2 (T1071.001 + T1573.002). Combines DDNS suffix matching, non-standard egress port, and beacon cadence to filter background noise.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count as conn_count, dc(_time) as distinct_minutes, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen, values(All_Traffic.src) as src_hosts, values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_ports, values(All_Traffic.app) as processes from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_is_internal=false All_Traffic.dest_port!=80 All_Traffic.dest_port!=443 All_Traffic.dest_port!=53 All_Traffic.dest_port!=8080 All_Traffic.dest_port!=8443 (All_Traffic.dest_host="*.ddns.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.hopto.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.sytes.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.zapto.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.myftp.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.myftp.biz" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.serveftp.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.servehttp.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.dynu.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.no-ip.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.no-ip.biz" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.changeip.com" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.dyndns.org" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.bounceme.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.redirectme.net" OR All_Traffic.dest_host="*.webhop.me") by All_Traffic.dest_host, All_Traffic.dest_port, _time span=1m | rename All_Traffic.* as * | stats sum(conn_count) as conn_count, dc(_time) as active_minutes, min(first_seen) as first_seen, max(last_seen) as last_seen, values(src_hosts) as src_hosts, values(processes) as processes by dest_host, dest_port | where conn_count>20 AND active_minutes>30 | sort - conn_count
ucs.kql:
let _ddns_suffixes = dynamic([".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".sytes.net",".zapto.org",".myftp.org",".myftp.biz",".serveftp.com",".servehttp.com",".servepics.com",".serveminecraft.net",".changeip.com",".dynu.net",".no-ip.org",".no-ip.biz",".dynamic-dns.net",".bounceme.net",".redirectme.net",".webhop.me",".dyndns.org"]);
let _standard_ports = dynamic([80, 443, 53, 8080, 8443]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemotePort !in (_standard_ports)
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (_ddns_suffixes)             // RedEcho-style DDNS abuse (T1568 + T1583.001)
| summarize ConnCount      = count(),
            ActiveMinutes  = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            FirstSeen      = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen       = max(Timestamp),
            Hosts          = make_set(DeviceName, 50),
            Ports          = make_set(RemotePort, 20),
            Processes      = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            SampleCmd      = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            SampleHash     = any(InitiatingProcessSHA256)
            by RemoteUrl, InitiatingProcessFileName
| where ConnCount > 20 and ActiveMinutes > 30          // sustained beacon, not bursty
| order by ConnCount desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _ddns_suffixes = dynamic([".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".sytes.net",".zapto.org",".myftp.org",".myftp.biz",".serveftp.com",".servehttp.com",".changeip.com",".dynu.net",".no-ip.org",".no-ip.biz",".bounceme.net",".redirectme.net",".webhop.me",".dyndns.org"]);
let _standard_ports = dynamic([80, 443, 53, 8080, 8443]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where DvcAction !in~ ("deny","drop","reject")
| where ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr) == false
| where DstPortNumber !in (_standard_ports)
| where isnotempty(DstHostname)
| where DstHostname has_any (_ddns_suffixes)
| summarize ConnCount     = count(),
            ActiveMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            FirstSeen     = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen      = max(TimeGenerated),
            Hosts         = make_set(SrcHostname, 50),
            Ports         = make_set(DstPortNumber, 20),
            Processes     = make_set(ActingProcessName, 10)
            by DstHostname, ActingProcessName
| where ConnCount > 20 and ActiveMinutes > 30
| order by ConnCount desc
ucs.rationale: Recorded Future's RedEcho reporting (Feb-2021 and Apr-2022) explicitly fingerprinted this group through ShadowPad C2 nodes registering DDNS hostnames and listening on non-standard TCP ports — the same trade-craft cluster shared with APT41/Barium. Generic 'beacon' detections fire on every monitoring agent; pinning the detection to (a) DDNS suffix list of providers actor-historically used, (b) non-s

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] RedEcho/ShadowPad signed-binary DLL side-loading from non-standard paths
ucs.description: ShadowPad — the implant tying RedEcho's intrusions together and shared with APT41/Winnti — is delivered via classic PRC-cluster DLL side-loading: a legitimately-signed AV/OEM/Microsoft executable (e.g., hpqhvsei.exe, BDReinit.exe, oleview.exe, RasTls.exe) dropped to ProgramData / Public / Temp alongside a hijacked DLL that the loader resolves first. Hunts the launch of a signed binary from a non-Program-Files / non-Windows folder.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen, values(Processes.process) as cmdlines, values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes, values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="hpqhvsei.exe" OR Processes.process_name="hpqhvind.exe" OR Processes.process_name="BDReinit.exe" OR Processes.process_name="oleview.exe" OR Processes.process_name="RasTls.exe" OR Processes.process_name="AvastSvc.exe" OR Processes.process_name="AvastBrowser.exe" OR Processes.process_name="mcods.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wsc_proxy.exe" OR Processes.process_name="TmDbgLog.exe" OR Processes.process_name="vmtoolsd.exe" OR Processes.process_name="DismHost.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") Processes.process_path!="*\\Program Files*" Processes.process_path!="C:\\Windows\\System32*" Processes.process_path!="C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64*" by Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name, Processes.process_path, Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | sort - first_seen
ucs.kql:
let _shadowpad_loader_bins = dynamic([
    "hpqhvsei.exe","hpqhvind.exe","BDReinit.exe","oleview.exe",
    "RasTls.exe","AvastSvc.exe","AvastBrowser.exe","mcods.exe",
    "wsc_proxy.exe","TmDbgLog.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","DismHost.exe",
    "Tcpz.exe","360ZipUpdate.exe","qbittorrent.exe"
]);
let _suspicious_dirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (_shadowpad_loader_bins)
| where FolderPath has_any (_suspicious_dirs)
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
   and  FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32"
   and  FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64"
| where InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel !in~ ("Low")     // exclude sandboxed previewers
// Pivot to the side-loaded DLL that landed adjacent
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | project DllTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId,
              SideLoadedDll=FileName, SideLoadedDllPath=FolderPath, SideLoadedDllSha256=SHA256
  ) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where SideLoadedDllPath =~ FolderPath                  // DLL adjacent to host binary
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AccountName,
          SignedHostBinary = FileName,
          SignedHostPath   = FolderPath,
          SignedHostHash   = SHA256,
          ParentProcess    = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd        = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          SideLoadedDll, SideLoadedDllPath, SideLoadedDllSha256,
          ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _shadowpad_loader_bins = dynamic([
    "hpqhvsei.exe","hpqhvind.exe","BDReinit.exe","oleview.exe",
    "RasTls.exe","AvastSvc.exe","AvastBrowser.exe","mcods.exe",
    "wsc_proxy.exe","TmDbgLog.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","DismHost.exe",
    "Tcpz.exe","360ZipUpdate.exe","qbittorrent.exe"
]);
let _suspicious_dirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\",@"\Users\Public\",@"\AppData\Local\Temp\",@"\PerfLogs\",@"\Windows\Temp\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where BinName in~ (_shadowpad_loader_bins)
| where NewProcessName has_any (_suspicious_dirs)
| where NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
   and  NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32"
   and  NewProcessName !startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          SignedHostBinary = BinName,
          SignedHostPath   = NewProcessName,
          ParentProcess    = ParentProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: RedEcho/ShadowPad Signed-Binary DLL Side-Loading from Non-Standard Paths
id: 6c4f1e21-9b1d-4d77-8c2a-5d9b7c6d9f01
status: experimental
description: Detects the RedEcho/APT41 cluster's ShadowPad side-loading pattern — a legitimately-signed AV/OEM/Microsoft executable launched from ProgramData, Public, Temp, PerfLogs or Windows\Temp instead of its true install path, used to load an adjacent hijacked DLL.
references:
  - https://www.recordedfuture.com/redecho-targeting-indian-power-sector
  - https://www.recordedfuture.com/redecho-modi-targeting-indian-power-sector-2022
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1042/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g1042
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\hpqhvsei.exe'
      - '\hpqhvind.exe'
      - '\BDReinit.exe'
      - '\oleview.exe'
      - '\RasTls.exe'
      - '\AvastSvc.exe'
      - '\AvastBrowser.exe'
      - '\mcods.exe'
      - '\wsc_proxy.exe'
      - '\TmDbgLog.exe'
      - '\vmtoolsd.exe'
      - '\DismHost.exe'
      - '\Tcpz.exe'
      - '\360ZipUpdate.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
  filter_legit_install:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and not filter_legit_install
falsepositives:
  - Portable AV/OEM utilities deliberately staged in ProgramData by IT
  - Software-deployment frameworks that cache installers in Temp
level: high
ucs.rationale: PRC-nexus clusters that share infrastructure with APT41 — the family RedEcho overlaps with — have used ShadowPad almost exclusively via DLL side-loading from signed third-party binaries (HP, Bitdefender, Avast, Trend Micro, McAfee, Symantec, VMware) since 2017. Generic 'unsigned DLL load' rules drown analysts; instead, this UC flips the logic: the *signed* binary is the anomaly, because adversarie
